The M365 Admin API is used by the Microsoft 365 Admin Center portal (admin.cloud.microsoft) for managing Microsoft 365 tenant settings, including:

  - Copilot Settings and Policies
  - Agent Management
  - Application Settings
  - Company and Organization Settings
  - Security Settings
  - Reports and Analytics
  - Search Configuration
  - Users and Groups
  - Tenant and Billing Management
  - Integrated Apps
  - Content Understanding
  - Viva Settings

These APIs are accessed through the admin portal and use session-based authentication with portal-specific headers.
They are used internally by the M365 Admin Center and are not officially documented by Microsoft.

**Architecture note:** Unlike the Defender XDR and Purview portals which share several backend services
(e.g., `/di/`, `/gws/`, `/medeina/`, `/msgraph/`), the M365 Admin Center uses its own isolated API layer
under `/admin/api/`. Some Microsoft Graph operations are proxied through `/fd/msgraph/` endpoints but
these use a separate federated proxy rather than the shared `/apiproxy/` layer used by XDR and Purview.

The following custom headers are required for most requests:

| Header | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `AjaxSessionKey` | Portal session key | (from portal bootstrap) |
| `x-portal-routekey` | Portal routing key | (from portal bootstrap) |
| `x-adminapp-request` | Admin app context path | `/homepage` |
| `x-ms-mac-appid` | Page-specific admin portal application ID | `050829af-7f24-4897-8f81-732bf47719ad` |
| `x-ms-mac-hostingapp` | Hosting application name | `M365AdminPortal` |

Live portal capture shows that `x-ms-mac-appid` and `x-ms-mac-version` vary by blade.
SharePoint-backed helpers such as Brand center also add a brokered SharePoint bearer token.

Documentation and specifications have been provided on a best efforts basis and may not be 100% accurate.
